paper_title: Geochronology and Thermochronology Methods in Southern Tibet

methods.description: This method involves the collection and analysis of zircon samples to confirm map unit identities, constrain sediment provenance, and determine the age of igneous intrusions using U-Pb dating techniques.

steps.description: Five igneous and five detrital zircon U-Pb geochronology samples were collected from map units across the study area.
steps.searchable_summary: collect zircon samples from study area

steps.description: Zircons were extracted from ~2 kg samples by crushing, followed by density and magnetic separation techniques.
steps.searchable_summary: extract zircons using crushing and separation

steps.description: For detrital samples, a large aliquot of grains (usually >1000) was mounted in epoxy together with crystal standards.
steps.searchable_summary: mount detrital zircon grains in epoxy

steps.description: For igneous samples, 20-50 selected zircon grains were mounted alongside crystal standards.
steps.searchable_summary: mount igneous zircon grains with standards

steps.description: Mounts were sanded to a depth of ~20 μm to expose zircon cores, polished, imaged with backscattered electron or cathodoluminescence techniques for navigation purposes, and then cleaned prior to analysis.
U-Pb geochronology was conducted by laser ablation-inductively coupled plasma-mass spectrometry (LA-ICP-MS) at the Arizona LaserChron center.
All samples were ablated using a Photon Machines Analyte G2 excimer laser equipped with a HelEx ablation cell, with a spot size ranging from 10 to 30 μm.
Signal intensities were measured with a secondary electron multiplier detector that operates in pulse-counting mode for signals less than 50,000 cps.
steps.searchable_summary: measure signal intensities with electron multiplier

steps.description: Crystallization ages for zircons were isolated from inherited ages in igneous U-Pb samples using the Arizona LaserChron Center in-house program AgePick.

methods.description: This method analyzes detrital zircon samples to track sediment provenance and interpret geological history in the context of the India-Asia collision.

steps.description: Detrital zircon samples from the Yarlung suture zone were analyzed to track sediment provenance prior to and during India-Asia collision.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze detrital zircon samples for provenance

steps.description: Comparison with a probability distribution function representative of Gangdese arc activity indicates derivation from Gangdese magmatic arc rocks.
steps.searchable_summary: compare age spectra with Gangdese arc activity

methods.description: This method compiles published thermochronologic data to analyze cooling ages and exhumation history across the Gangdese Mountains and Yarlung suture zone.

steps.description: Published medium-to low-temperature thermochronologic data were compiled for the Gangdese batholith, Kailas Formation, and Liuqu Formation.
The proximity of thermochronology samples to the Yarlung suture was calculated and plotted against thermochronological age to reveal trends.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate and plot sample proximity to Yarlung suture
